description: pyrdp is a Python RDP man-in-the-middle and library for interacting with RDP services. Can be used to record sessions, MITM, and extract credentials.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/pyrdp && python3 pyrdp.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/pyrdp && python3 pyrdp.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/pyrdp && python3 pyrdp.py --help`. RDP man-in-the-middle and library.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the post_exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: RDP man-in-the-middle and library.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: RDP man-in-the-middle and library.

name: pyrdp

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
